text.title: hybrid-search-basics Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for hybrid-search-basics. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Use Reciprocal Rank Fusion (RRF) as the default fusion strategy. Use linear score normalization only after benchmarking shows improvement on the specific corpus.
rule.rationale: RRF operates on rank positions rather than raw scores, making it robust to scale differences between BM25 (unbounded, corpus-dependent) and cosine similarity (0–1). Linear fusion requires careful per-corpus normalization to avoid BM25 scores dominating.

rule.statement: Never compare raw BM25 scores across different corpora or index configurations.
rule.rationale: BM25 scores are distribution-dependent. A score of 10 on a small corpus is not comparable to a score of 10 on a large corpus. Always normalize BM25 within the same retrieval call before fusion.

rule.statement: Start alpha at 0.5 (50% semantic / 50% keyword) and tune per domain using a labeled evaluation set of at least 50 queries.
rule.rationale: There is no universally optimal alpha. Technical corpora with many exact identifiers tend toward lower alpha (more keyword weight). Prose-heavy corpora tend toward higher alpha (more semantic weight).

rule.statement: Log the alpha value used per query in production to build a calibration dataset for automated tuning.
rule.rationale: Dynamic alpha selection improves retrieval quality but requires empirical data. Without logging, there is no basis for improving the alpha selection heuristics over time.

rule.statement: Use the vector database's native hybrid search endpoint when available (Qdrant, Weaviate, Elasticsearch) rather than calling two endpoints and fusing client-side.
rule.rationale: Native hybrid search executes both retrievers in a single server-side call, reducing latency. Client-side fusion requires two round-trips and local merge logic.

rule.statement: For Qdrant sparse vectors, pre-compute BM25 or SPLADE sparse encodings before insert. You cannot pass raw text to a sparse vector endpoint.
rule.rationale: Qdrant's sparse vector format requires pre-encoded sparse representations. Attempting to pass tokenized text directly produces a type error at the API level.

rule.statement: Apply domain-specific stop words for BM25. Generic English stop words hurt technical corpora where words like "the" may appear in product names.
rule.rationale: Standard stop word lists remove common words that may carry meaning in technical domains. For example, "The Architect" as a product name would lose its key token.

rule.statement: Rebuild the BM25 index when documents are added incrementally. Some vector databases do not support live BM25 updates.
rule.rationale: BM25 IDF scores are corpus-wide statistics. Adding documents without rebuilding the BM25 component produces stale IDF values that degrade keyword scoring for both old and new documents.

rule.statement: Compute alpha before the retrieval call, not after. An agent that decides alpha post-retrieval cannot change the already-returned result set.
rule.rationale: Alpha determines the retrieval mix at query time. It must be computed from the query characteristics before the retrieval call is made.

rule.statement: Start alpha at 0.5 and tune per domain using a labeled evaluation set of 50+ queries.
rule.rationale: Equal weighting is the starting point, but domain-specific tuning dramatically improves quality.

rule.statement: Use RRF over linear score normalization by default — it is more robust to scale differences between BM25 and cosine scores.
rule.rationale: RRF eliminates the need for per-corpus score normalization, which is error-prone and corpus-dependent.

rule.statement: Apply BM25 domain-specific stop words; generic English stop words hurt technical corpora.
rule.rationale: Technical domains have product names and identifiers that overlap with common English stop words.

rule.statement: Run vector search and BM25 independently in parallel, then fuse — do not rely on the database to do this unless the DB offers native hybrid.
rule.rationale: Parallel execution reduces latency. Many databases do not offer native hybrid, making client-side fusion and parallelization necessary.

rule.statement: Normalize vectors before cosine scoring; un-normalized vectors produce incorrect similarity scores with dot-product indices.
rule.rationale: Unit-normalized vectors allow dot-product to equal cosine similarity, which is faster.
